sources.name: Bar chart of user enrolments
sources.description: Count of distinct users holding each role site-wide, rendered as a bar chart (x = role shortname, y = user count). Roles with no users are dropped.
sources.querysql:
SELECT r.shortname, COUNT(DISTINCT ra.userid) AS usercount
FROM {role} r
JOIN {role_assignments} ra ON r.id = ra.roleid
GROUP BY r.shortname
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT ra.userid) > 0
LIMIT 100

sources.name: Course enrolments for current user
sources.description: show user id field and course shortname the user is enrolled on
sources.querysql: SELECT ue.userid, CONCAT('', c.shortname, '') AS course FROM {user_enrolments} ue JOIN {enrol} e ON ue.enrolid = e.id JOIN {course} c ON e.courseid = c.id

sources.name: Distinct enrolments per category and sub categories: demonstrates %%CONTEXT_COURSE%%
sources.description: Count number of distinct learners and teachers enrolled per category (including all its sub categories)
sources.querysql:
SELECT
    COUNT(DISTINCT lra.userid) AS learners,
    COUNT(DISTINCT tra.userid) AS teachers
FROM
    {course} c
    LEFT JOIN {context} ctx ON c.id = ctx.instanceid AND ctx.contextlevel = %%CONTEXT_COURSE%%
    JOIN {role_assignments} lra ON lra.contextid = ctx.id
    JOIN {role_assignments} tra ON tra.contextid = ctx.id
    JOIN {course_categories} cats ON c.category = cats.id
WHERE
    cats.path LIKE CONCAT('%/', (SELECT id FROM {course_categories} WHERE name = 'Category 1' LIMIT 1), '/%')
    AND lra.roleid = (SELECT id FROM {role} WHERE shortname = 'student')
    OR tra.roleid = (SELECT id FROM {role} WHERE shortname = 'editingteacher')

sources.name: Enrolled students in current course: demonstrates %%TIMESTAMP and %%COURSECONTEXT
sources.description: List Students with enrollment and completion dates in current course. Demonstrates %%TIMESTAMP allowing for queries that will work with both MySQL and Postgres
sources.querysql:
SELECT
    u.firstname,
    u.lastname,
    u.idnumber,
    u.institution,
    u.department,
    u.email,
    %%TIMESTAMP(cc.timeenrolled)%% AS timeenrolled,
    %%TIMESTAMP(cc.timecompleted)%% AS timecompleted
FROM {role_assignments} AS ra
JOIN {context} AS ctx ON ctx.id = ra.contextid AND ctx.id = %%COURSECONTEXT%%
JOIN {course} AS c ON c.id = ctx.instanceid AND c.id = %%COURSEID%%
JOIN {user} AS u ON u.id = ra.userid
JOIN {course_completions} AS cc ON cc.course = c.id AND cc.userid = u.id
LIMIT 100

sources.name: Enrolled users who have never accessed a given course
sources.description: Users enrolled in a course who have no last-access record for it — i.e. never opened the course. The course is hard-coded as en.courseid = 2; edit that id for your course.
sources.querysql:
SELECT username, firstname, lastname, idnumber
FROM {user_enrolments} ue
JOIN {enrol}           en ON ue.enrolid = en.id
JOIN {user}            uu ON uu.id      = ue.userid
WHERE en.courseid = 2
AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT * FROM {user_lastaccess} la
    WHERE la.userid = ue.userid
    AND la.courseid = en.courseid
)

sources.name: Line graph of log writes per day
sources.description: Report over logstore_standard_log.
sources.querysql:
SELECT userid, (timecreated - (timecreated % 86400)) AS day, COUNT(*) AS writes
FROM {logstore_standard_log}
GROUP BY userid, (timecreated - (timecreated % 86400))
ORDER BY userid, day

sources.name: Lists "loggedin users" from the last 120 days demonstrates %%NOW%%
sources.description: The %%NOW%% token gets the current time and will worth with both Postgres and MySQL

sources.name: Most Active courses
sources.description: Courses ranked by log-event count within a fixed date window, demonstrating %%EPOCH%% to bound a timestamp range portably across MySQL and Postgres. Site course (id 1) excluded.
sources.querysql:
SELECT
    c.fullname AS Course,
    COUNT(l.userid) AS Views
FROM {logstore_standard_log} l
JOIN {course} c ON c.id = l.courseid
WHERE l.timecreated >  %%EPOCH('2015-01-01 00:00:00')%%
  AND l.timecreated <= %%EPOCH('2027-06-30 23:59:59')%%
  AND l.courseid > 1
GROUP BY c.id, c.fullname
ORDER BY Views DESC

sources.name: Pie chart of user enrolments
sources.description: Count of distinct users holding each role site-wide, rendered as a pie chart (slices = role shortname, size = user count). Roles with no users are dropped.
sources.querysql:
SELECT r.shortname, COUNT(DISTINCT ra.userid) AS usercount
FROM {role} r
JOIN {role_assignments} ra ON r.id = ra.roleid
GROUP BY r.shortname
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT ra.userid) > 0
LIMIT 100

sources.name: Student (user) Count in each Course
sources.description: Report over role_assignments, context, user, course.
sources.querysql: SELECT CONCAT('', c.fullname, '') AS Course, CONCAT('Show users') AS users, COUNT(DISTINCT u.id) AS students FROM {role_assignments} asg JOIN {context} ctx ON asg.contextid = ctx.id AND ctx.contextlevel = %%CONTEXT_COURSE%% JOIN {user} u ON u.id = asg.userid JOIN {course} c ON ctx.instanceid = c.id WHERE asg.roleid = (SELECT id FROM {role} WHERE shortname = 'student') GROUP BY c.id, c.fullname ORDER BY Students DESC LIMIT 100

sources.name: Course activity for courses you teach: demonstrates the teacher-course filter
sources.description: Log events per course. After importing, publish this query, then in the edit form set "Restrict to courses the viewer teaches" to the courseid column. Publish to a wide audience (e.g. all staff): each teacher then sees only the courses they teach, and a user who teaches nothing sees no rows.
sources.querysql: SELECT c.id AS courseid, CONCAT('', c.shortname, '') AS course, COUNT(l.id) AS events FROM {course} c JOIN {logstore_standard_log} l ON l.courseid = c.id WHERE c.id > 1 GROUP BY c.id, c.shortname ORDER BY events DESC LIMIT 200

sources.name: Course grades visible only to teachers: demonstrates the Course role audience
sources.description: Final grades for every student in the scoped course. After importing, set the query's course (which binds %%COURSEID%%), then in the edit form set Audience to "Course role" and pick Teacher / Non-editing teacher. Publish: teachers in that course see all students' grades, students see nothing (blocked by the report's audience). Audience config is not portable, so it must be set by hand after import.
sources.querysql:
SELECT
    u.id AS userid,
    u.firstname,
    u.lastname,
    gi.itemname AS item,
    gg.finalgrade AS grade,
    %%TIMESTAMP(gg.timemodified, dd/mm/yyyy)%% AS graded
FROM {user} u
JOIN {grade_grades} gg ON gg.userid = u.id
JOIN {grade_items} gi ON gi.id = gg.itemid
WHERE gi.courseid = %%COURSEID%%
  AND gi.itemtype = 'mod'
ORDER BY u.lastname, gi.itemname
LIMIT 200

sources.name: Per-course enrolment counts for the hosting block: demonstrates the page-course filter
sources.description: Enrolled-user count per course. The backing query is generic (no fixed course), so the VIEW lists every course. After importing, publish this query, then in the edit form set "Restrict to the course the block is on" to the courseid column. Add the Report sources block to a course (or to every course via 'Add to all course pages'): each block then shows only the row for the course of the page it sits on, so one published query feeds every course its own enrolment count. Outside a course page (Dashboard / site home) the filter is skipped and all rows show. This setting is not portable, so it must be set by hand after import.
sources.querysql: SELECT c.id AS courseid, CONCAT('', c.shortname, '') AS course, COUNT(DISTINCT ue.userid) AS enrolments FROM {course} c JOIN {enrol} e ON e.courseid = c.id JOIN {user_enrolments} ue ON ue.enrolid = e.id WHERE c.id > 1 GROUP BY c.id, c.shortname ORDER BY enrolments DESC LIMIT 200

sources.name: All badges available in the system, with earned count
sources.description: Every badge defined on the site with its scope (System / Course), the course it belongs to (or * for site-wide), whether it is currently available, and how many times it has been issued. Demonstrates correlated subqueries and CASE decoding of the badge type/status columns.
sources.querysql:
SELECT
    b.id,
    b.name,
    CASE WHEN b.type = 1 THEN 'System' WHEN b.type = 2 THEN 'Course' END AS context,
    CASE WHEN b.courseid IS NOT NULL
         THEN (SELECT c.shortname FROM {course} c WHERE c.id = b.courseid)
         ELSE '*' END AS course,
    CASE WHEN b.status IN (1, 3) THEN 'Yes' WHEN b.status = 4 THEN 'x' ELSE 'No' END AS available,
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {badge_issued} d WHERE d.badgeid = b.id) AS earned
FROM {badge} b
ORDER BY b.name
LIMIT 200

sources.name: All badges issued, by user
sources.description: Each badge award: the recipient, badge name, scope, how the badge was earned (decoded from criteria type/method), the issue date and a link to the public badge page. Demonstrates %%TIMESTAMP%% date formatting, CASE decoding and a %%WWWROOT%% link built with chr(63) for the ? character.
sources.querysql: SELECT u.username, b.name AS badgename, CASE WHEN b.courseid IS NOT NULL THEN (SELECT c.shortname FROM {course} c WHERE c.id = b.courseid) ELSE '*' END AS context, CASE WHEN t.criteriatype = 1 AND t.method = 1 THEN 'Activity Completion (All)' WHEN t.criteriatype = 1 AND t.method = 2 THEN 'Activity Completion (Any)' WHEN t.criteriatype = 2 AND t.method = 2 THEN 'Manual Award' WHEN t.criteriatype = 4 AND t.method = 1 THEN 'Course Completion (All)' WHEN t.criteriatype = 4 AND t.method = 2 THEN 'Course Completion (Any)' ELSE CONCAT('Other: ', t.criteriatype) END AS criteriatype, %%TIMESTAMP(d.dateissued, yyyy-mm-dd)%% AS dateissued, CONCAT('link') AS details FROM {badge_issued} d JOIN {badge} b ON d.badgeid = b.id JOIN {user} u ON d.userid = u.id JOIN {badge_criteria} t ON b.id = t.badgeid WHERE t.criteriatype <> 0 ORDER BY u.username LIMIT 200

sources.name: Cohorts by user
sources.description: Lists every cohort membership site-wide: the member's name alongside the cohort id number and name. A simple three-table join over the cohort subsystem.
sources.querysql:
SELECT u.firstname, u.lastname, h.idnumber, h.name
FROM {cohort} h
JOIN {cohort_members} hm ON h.id = hm.cohortid
JOIN {user} u ON hm.userid = u.id
ORDER BY u.firstname
LIMIT 200

sources.name: Courses that use groups
sources.description: Courses with group mode enabled, showing the group mode, a count of activity modules and a count of groups. The course name links to the course's groups page. Demonstrates correlated count subqueries.
sources.querysql: SELECT CONCAT('', c.fullname, '') AS course, c.groupmode, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {course_modules} cm WHERE cm.course = c.id) AS modules, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {groups} g WHERE g.courseid = c.id) AS numgroups FROM {course} c WHERE c.groupmode > 0 ORDER BY c.fullname LIMIT 200

sources.name: Site-wide completed SCORM activities by course
sources.description: Every completed SCORM attempt across the site: learner, course, attempt number, status and the completion date. Rewritten for the Moodle 5 SCORM schema (scorm_attempt / scorm_scoes_value / scorm_element); matches both SCORM 1.2 (cmi.core.lesson_status) and SCORM 2004 (cmi.completion_status).
sources.querysql:
SELECT
    u.firstname,
    u.lastname,
    c.fullname AS course,
    sa.attempt,
    sv.value AS status,
    %%TIMESTAMP(sv.timemodified, mm-dd-yyyy)%% AS completed
FROM {scorm_scoes_value} sv
JOIN {scorm_element} se ON se.id = sv.elementid AND se.element IN ('cmi.completion_status', 'cmi.core.lesson_status')
JOIN {scorm_attempt} sa ON sa.id = sv.attemptid
JOIN {scorm} sc ON sc.id = sa.scormid
JOIN {course} c ON c.id = sc.course
JOIN {user} u ON u.id = sa.userid
WHERE sv.value = 'completed'
ORDER BY c.fullname, u.lastname, u.firstname, sa.attempt
LIMIT 200

sources.name: Log actions per teaching/learning role
sources.description: Counts log events grouped by role (editing teacher, teacher, student) and action, over the standard log store. Demonstrates %%CONTEXT_COURSE%% and selecting roles by shortname rather than hard-coded role ids. Reads the Moodle 5 logstore_standard_log (the old mdl_log table is gone).
sources.querysql:
SELECT r.shortname, l.action, COUNT(l.userid) AS counter
FROM {logstore_standard_log} l
JOIN {context} ctx ON ctx.instanceid = l.courseid AND ctx.contextlevel = %%CONTEXT_COURSE%%
JOIN {role_assignments} ra ON l.userid = ra.userid AND ra.contextid = ctx.id
JOIN {role} r ON ra.roleid = r.id
WHERE r.shortname IN ('editingteacher', 'teacher', 'student')
GROUP BY r.shortname, l.action
ORDER BY counter DESC
LIMIT 200

sources.name: All site users by course enrolment, with role
sources.description: Every course enrolment site-wide: the user, the course they are enrolled in, and their role in that course (resolved from role assignments at course context, so it reflects the real assigned role rather than the enrolment default).
sources.querysql:
SELECT
    u.firstname,
    u.lastname,
    u.email,
    c.fullname AS course,
    r.shortname AS role,
    r.name AS rolename
FROM {course} c
JOIN {enrol} e ON e.courseid = c.id
JOIN {user_enrolments} ue ON ue.enrolid = e.id
JOIN {user} u ON ue.userid = u.id
LEFT JOIN {context} ctx ON ctx.instanceid = c.id AND ctx.contextlevel = %%CONTEXT_COURSE%%
LEFT JOIN {role_assignments} ra ON ra.userid = u.id AND ra.contextid = ctx.id
LEFT JOIN {role} r ON r.id = ra.roleid
ORDER BY c.fullname, u.lastname
LIMIT 200

sources.name: Users enrolled for more than 4 weeks
sources.description: Enrolments older than 28 days, showing the user, course and the date the enrolment was created. Demonstrates arithmetic on the %%NOW%% token (current epoch minus 28 days) in place of the MySQL-only DATEDIFF/NOW functions, so it runs on both MySQL and Postgres.
sources.querysql:
SELECT
    u.id AS userid,
    u.username,
    c.shortname AS course,
    %%TIMESTAMP(ue.timecreated, yyyy-mm-dd)%% AS enrolledsince
FROM {user_enrolments} ue
JOIN {enrol} e ON e.id = ue.enrolid
JOIN {course} c ON c.id = e.courseid
JOIN {user} u ON u.id = ue.userid
WHERE ue.timecreated < %%NOW%% - (28 * 86400)
ORDER BY ue.timecreated
LIMIT 200

sources.name: Users who have logged in only once
sources.description: Accounts that have a current login recorded but no previous login (lastlogin = 0) and have accessed the site at least once. A lightweight starter query with no joins.
sources.querysql:
SELECT id, username, firstname, lastname, idnumber
FROM {user}
WHERE deleted = 0
  AND lastlogin = 0
  AND lastaccess > 0
LIMIT 200

sources.name: Site-wide grade report (course totals and items)
sources.description: Final grades across every course: user, course, category, grade item (course totals are labelled '<course> - Total') and the date graded. Demonstrates %%CONTEXT_COURSE%% to scope role assignments and %%TIMESTAMP%% for the graded date.
sources.querysql:
SELECT
    u.firstname,
    u.lastname,
    c.fullname AS course,
    cc.name AS category,
    CASE WHEN gi.itemtype = 'course' THEN CONCAT(c.fullname, ' - Total') ELSE gi.itemname END AS item,
    ROUND(gg.finalgrade, 2) AS grade,
    %%TIMESTAMP(gg.timemodified, yyyy-mm-dd)%% AS graded
FROM {course} c
JOIN {context} ctx ON c.id = ctx.instanceid AND ctx.contextlevel = %%CONTEXT_COURSE%%
JOIN {role_assignments} ra ON ra.contextid = ctx.id
JOIN {user} u ON u.id = ra.userid
JOIN {grade_grades} gg ON gg.userid = u.id
JOIN {grade_items} gi ON gi.id = gg.itemid
JOIN {course_categories} cc ON cc.id = c.category
WHERE gi.courseid = c.id
ORDER BY u.lastname
LIMIT 200

sources.name: Enrolments grouped by user: demonstrates the control-break grouped view
sources.description: One band per user (name), then a detail row per course they are enrolled on with the enrolment date. Set the grouped view's break column to userid to see the master-detail layout on screen and in CSV/Excel exports.
sources.querysql:
SELECT
    u.id AS userid,
    u.firstname,
    u.lastname,
    c.shortname AS course,
    c.fullname AS coursename,
    %%TIMESTAMP(ue.timecreated)%% AS enrolled
FROM {user_enrolments} ue
JOIN {enrol} e ON ue.enrolid = e.id
JOIN {course} c ON e.courseid = c.id
JOIN {user} u ON u.id = ue.userid
WHERE u.deleted = 0 AND c.id > 1
LIMIT 500
